round_note: Refire on 2026-04-27. This witness supersedes the earlier same-path witness because the repo now contains a real hexa `core/agent` scaffold; the old premise 'zero non-TS analog exists' is no longer true. The hexa witness harness was re-run again in this turn and still passes 20/20, so the partial-positive/full-negative ROI split remains current rather than inherited.

alpha.consumed_how: framed the decision as delta-preserving subset extraction versus whole-class transliteration

beta.consumed_how: forced the cycle to score realized partial-port before revisiting the legacy no-port default

gamma.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_lhc invocation=tool/lhc_opendata_bridge.hexa (real fetch deferred to sub-agent or OFFLINE-FALLBACK)
gamma.consumed_how: reinforced staged-port logic: land the minimal measurable slice first, keep the high-energy async/runtime layers out until their substrate exists

core_5op_kernel: 3) fan out K>=3 orthogonal axes (raw 48 correlation < 0.7)
5) emit witness JSON to declared landing path

load_bearing_runtime_state_evidence: extension runner lifecycle and event emission
compaction, retry, branching, HTML export, bash execution

files.role: minimal constructor with default tool set

realized_hexa_port.declared_scope_in_headers: P1 backend port; intentionally minimal; mutable runtime state, provider port, tool execution, compaction, and extension runners are deferred

intake.premise_correction: The old negative-ROI witness overfit to a then-true condition: no non-TS analog existed. That condition is now false. A non-TS backend scaffold exists, is executable, and is locally tested.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: non_ts_realization_absent
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: If no hexa analog exists in-tree, any positive ROI claim is speculative only.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: partial_port_is_unbounded
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: If the hexa port claims broad parity without explicitly bounded scope, ROI is dishonest.
pre_registered_falsifiers.evidence: session.hexa header narrows scope to identity + conversation; dispatch.hexa declares STUB status; bootstrap.hexa defers auth/model-registry/extensions

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: full_class_now_cheap
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: If the remaining TS-only surface has become low-coupling and low-runtime-friction, a full port may now be positive ROI.
pre_registered_falsifiers.evidence: 3110 LOC TS reference still carries 25 relative imports, Promise/AbortController state, compaction, extension runner, session tree navigation, HTML export, and bash execution

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: hexa_scaffold_unverified
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: If the realized port has no runnable witness, realized ROI is not credible.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: axis_floor_not_met
pre_registered_falsifiers.evidence: K=4; realization-status, scope-boundary, full-port-negative, and partial-port-positive are distinct

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: feature_creep_smuggled_in
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: If the recommendation quietly expands from session-core to provider/tool/extension subsystems, ROI reasoning is invalid.
pre_registered_falsifiers.evidence: recommendation stays confined to already-landed P1 slice plus explicit next seams; no claim of full behavioral parity

axes.name: realization-status
axes.finding: Positive for partial ROI. The repo now owns a real hexa session scaffold with constructor, append/register helpers, ready-line formatter, dispatch seam, runnable main entry, and a passing witness harness.

axes.name: scope-boundary
axes.finding: The positive ROI is narrow, not general. 217 hexa LOC captures only identity + conversation baseline, about 7% of the TS reference by LOC. The scaffold deliberately excludes async/event/provider/extension/compaction layers.

axes.name: full-port-negative
axes.finding: Whole-file port remains negative ROI today. `agent-session.ts` is still the package kernel for interactive, print, rpc, and server paths, with Promise-based event queues, multiple AbortControllers, and tight coupling to TS-native dependencies and managers.

axes.name: partial-port-positive
axes.finding: The already-landed hexa slice is positive ROI because it created a stable backend foothold at low cost, with explicit non-parity disclaimers and a runnable witness. This is a successful bounded port, not evidence that the remaining 2893 TS LOC should follow immediately.

candidate_options.name: Preserve the old blanket NO-PORT verdict
candidate_options.reason: stale premise; a realized hexa port now exists

candidate_options.name: Keep the landed P1 hexa scaffold and treat it as positive realized ROI
candidate_options.reason: bounded scope, passing witness, multiple live hexa consumers

candidate_options.name: Continue porting most of `agent-session.ts` into hexa now
candidate_options.reason: remaining surface is still dominated by TS-native async/provider/extension/runtime concerns

candidate_options.name: Advance only by adjacent seams after their substrate lands
candidate_options.reason: best next ROI frontier is narrow follow-on seams such as message typing or provider-independent state helpers, not the whole class

tier_1_promotions.claim: The port ROI is now partially realized and positive for the P1 backend scaffold. `core/agent/{session,bootstrap,dispatch}.hexa` plus `test_session.hexa` demonstrate that the identity + conversation slice was worth porting.
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_pass_pattern: passes F1, F2, F4, F5, F6

tier_1_promotions.claim: A full `agent-session.ts` port remains negative ROI today. The remaining TS surface is still the load-bearing runtime kernel for async eventing, compaction, extension lifecycles, session-tree operations, HTML export, and bash execution.

The decision frontier has shifted from 'should anything be ported?' to 'which next seam is admissible after the scaffold?' The right follow-on is staged seam extraction gated by substrate readiness, not whole-class translation.

roi_verdict.summary: The repo now contains a real, tested hexa `AgentSession` scaffold, so the old blanket NO-PORT conclusion is obsolete. That said, the realized gain is deliberately narrow: about 217 LOC of identity/conversation/bootstrap/dispatch scaffolding versus a 3110 LOC TS kernel. Result: the bounded P1 port has positive ROI, while porting the rest of `agent-session.ts` still does not.

next_round_handoff.task: Re-run this cycle only when one deferred substrate lands: provider port, tool-dispatch port, extension runner port, or compaction/session-tree port.
If a next seam is chosen, require the same P1 discipline: header-declared subset, runnable hexa witness, and no silent parity claims.
Do not schedule a wholesale class port until the TS-only runtime concerns materially shrink or gain direct non-TS consumers.

read agent-session.ts header and exported surface
read session.hexa, bootstrap.hexa, dispatch.hexa, main.hexa, test_session.hexa
rg over hive/core and nexus confirming hexa agent-session consumers and absence of a broader parity port

honesty_triad.no_overreach: This witness does not claim that the hexa scaffold matches TS behavior beyond the explicitly documented P1 slice.
honesty_triad.write_barrier: Only this witness JSON was modified in this cycle.

summary.headline: The old witness is stale. A real hexa `core/agent` scaffold now exists and passes its witness harness, so partial port ROI has already been captured. The remaining full-class TS surface is still too coupled and runtime-heavy to justify a wholesale port.
